sol_sql:
/* 
Intent: Analyze signal quality (SNQI) with atmospheric conditions using window functions
Knowledge Used: SNQI (id:0)
Advanced Features: Window functions, conditional aggregation
*/
WITH signal_quality AS (
    -- Step 1: Calculate SNQI for all signals with their observation conditions
    SELECT 
        s.SignalRegistry,
        s.SnrRatio - 0.1 * ABS(s.NoiseFloorDbm) AS SNQI,
        o.WeathProfile
    FROM Signals s
    JOIN Telescopes t ON s.TelescRef = t.TelescRegistry
    JOIN Observatories o ON t.ObservStation = o.ObservStation
)
-- Step 2: Rank signals by quality within weather profiles
SELECT 
    WeathProfile,
    AVG(SNQI) AS avg_snqi,
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY SNQI) AS median_snqi,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE SNQI > 0) AS analyzable_signals
FROM signal_quality
GROUP BY WeathProfile
ORDER BY avg_snqi DESC;

follow_up.sol_sql:
/*
Intent: Analyze Atmospheric Observability Index (AOI) with weather conditions
Knowledge Used: AOI (id:1)
Advanced Features: Window functions, conditional aggregation
*/

WITH observ_conditions AS (
    -- Step 1: Calculate AOI for all signals with their observation conditions
    SELECT 
        s.SignalRegistry,
        o.AtmosTransparency * (1 - o.HumidityRate / 100) * (1 - 0.02 * o.WindSpeedMs) AS AOI,
        o.WeathProfile
    FROM 
        Signals s
    JOIN 
        Telescopes t ON s.TelescRef = t.TelescRegistry
    JOIN 
        Observatories o ON t.ObservStation = o.ObservStation
)

-- Step 2: Aggregate AOI metrics by weather profile
SELECT 
    WeathProfile,
    AVG(AOI) AS avg_aoi,
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY AOI) AS median_aoi,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE AOI > 0) AS valid_observations
FROM 
    observ_conditions
GROUP BY 
    WeathProfile
ORDER BY 
    avg_aoi DESC;

sol_sql:
/*
Intent: Analyze distribution of technosignature probabilities using statistical functions
Knowledge Used: TOLS (id:3)
Advanced Features: Statistical functions, CASE expressions
*/
-- Step 1: Calculate TOLS for all signals with probability data
SELECT 
    -- Step 2: Bin the TOLS values into categories
    CASE 
        WHEN p.TechSigProb * (1 - p.NatSrcProb) * p.SigUnique * (0.5 + p.AnomScore/10) < 0.25 THEN 'Low'
        WHEN p.TechSigProb * (1 - p.NatSrcProb) * p.SigUnique * (0.5 + p.AnomScore/10) < 0.75 THEN 'Medium'
        ELSE 'High'
    END AS tol_category,
    -- Step 3: Calculate statistics for each category
    COUNT(*) AS signal_count,
    AVG(s.BwHz/(s.CenterFreqMhz * 1000000)) AS avg_bfr,
    STDDEV(p.AnomScore) AS anomaly_stddev
FROM Signals s
JOIN SignalProbabilities p ON s.SignalRegistry = p.SignalRef
GROUP BY tol_category;

follow_up.sol_sql:
/*
Intent: Drill down into the TOLS category with the highest signal count to analyze signal stability
Knowledge Used: TOLS (id:3), TOLS Category (id:52), SSM (id:7)
Advanced Features: Subquery to identify the category with the most signals, statistical functions
*/
WITH tol_counts AS (
    -- Step 1: Recompute the first-round query to find the category with the most signals
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN p.TechSigProb * (1 - p.NatSrcProb) * p.SigUnique * (0.5 + p.AnomScore/10) < 0.25 THEN 'Low'
            WHEN p.TechSigProb * (1 - p.NatSrcProb) * p.SigUnique * (0.5 + p.AnomScore/10) < 0.75 THEN 'Medium'
            ELSE 'High'
        END AS tol_category,
        COUNT(*) AS signal_count
    FROM Signals s
    JOIN SignalProbabilities p ON s.SignalRegistry = p.SignalRef
    GROUP BY tol_category
    ORDER BY signal_count DESC
    LIMIT 1
),
signal_stability AS (
    -- Step 2: Calculate SSM for signals in the top category
    SELECT 
        s.SignalRegistry,
        (1 - ABS(s.FreqDriftHzs) / (s.FreqMhz * 1000)) * (s.SigDurSec / (1 + s.DoppShiftHz / 1000)) AS ssm
    FROM Signals s
    JOIN SignalProbabilities p ON s.SignalRegistry = p.SignalRef
    JOIN tol_counts tc ON 
        CASE 
            WHEN p.TechSigProb * (1 - p.NatSrcProb) * p.SigUnique * (0.5 + p.AnomScore/10) < 0.25 THEN 'Low'
            WHEN p.TechSigProb * (1 - p.NatSrcProb) * p.SigUnique * (0.5 + p.AnomScore/10) < 0.75 THEN 'Medium'
            ELSE 'High'
        END = tc.tol_category
)
-- Step 3: Aggregate SSM statistics for the top category
SELECT 
    AVG(ssm) AS avg_ssm,
    MIN(ssm) AS min_ssm,
    MAX(ssm) AS max_ssm
FROM signal_stability;

sol_sql:
/*
Intent: Quantify lunar interference impact using LIF calculation
Knowledge Used: LIF (id:9)
Advanced Features: Date functions, lateral join
*/
-- Step 1: Calculate LIF for all observations with lunar data
SELECT 
    o.ObservStation,
    -- Step 2: Calculate average LIF by moon phase
    o.LunarStage,
    AVG((1 - o.LunarDistDeg/180) * (1 - o.AtmosTransparency)) AS avg_lif,
    -- Step 3: Count high interference events
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE (1 - o.LunarDistDeg/180) * (1 - o.AtmosTransparency) > 0.5) AS high_interf_count
FROM Observatories o
GROUP BY o.ObservStation, o.LunarStage
ORDER BY avg_lif DESC;

follow_up.sol_sql:
/*
Intent: Quantify lunar interference impact by switching grouping to geomagnetic status
Knowledge Used: LIF (id:9)
Advanced Features: Date functions, lateral join
*/
-- Step 1: Calculate LIF for all observations with lunar data
SELECT 
    o.ObservStation,
    -- Step 2: Calculate average LIF by geomagnetic status instead of lunar stage
    o.GeomagStatus,
    AVG((1 - o.LunarDistDeg/180) * (1 - o.AtmosTransparency)) AS avg_lif,
    -- Step 3: Count high interference events
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE (1 - o.LunarDistDeg/180) * (1 - o.AtmosTransparency) > 0.5) AS high_interf_count
FROM Observatories o
GROUP BY o.ObservStation, o.GeomagStatus
ORDER BY avg_lif DESC;

sol_sql:
/*
Intent: Evaluate detection efficiency for potential technosignatures
Knowledge Used: Technosignature (id:10), TOLS (id:3)
Advanced Features: Common Table Expressions, complex filtering
*/
WITH tech_signals AS (
    SELECT 
        s.SignalRegistry,
        s.TelescRef,
        p.TechSigProb * (1 - p.NatSrcProb) * p.SigUnique * (0.5 + p.AnomScore/10) AS TOLS,
        s.BwHz/(s.CenterFreqMhz * 1000000) AS BFR
    FROM Signals s
    JOIN SignalProbabilities p ON s.SignalRegistry = p.SignalRef
    WHERE p.TechSigProb > 0.7 
      AND p.NatSrcProb < 0.3 
      AND p.ArtSrcProb < 50
      AND s.BwHz/(s.CenterFreqMhz * 1000000) < 0.001
)
SELECT 
    t.ObservStation,
    COUNT(*) AS potential_tech_signals,
    AVG(ts.TOLS) AS avg_tols,
    AVG(ts.BFR) AS avg_bfr,
    COUNT(*) * 100.0 / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tech_signals) AS percentage_of_total
FROM tech_signals ts
JOIN Telescopes t ON ts.TelescRef = t.TelescRegistry
GROUP BY t.ObservStation
ORDER BY potential_tech_signals DESC;

follow_up.sol_sql:
/*
Intent: Evaluate detection efficiency for potential technosignatures with modified metrics
Knowledge Used: Technosignature (id:10), SNQI (id:0), ECI (id:6)
Advanced Features: Common Table Expressions, complex filtering
*/
WITH tech_signals AS (
    SELECT 
        s.SignalRegistry,
        s.TelescRef,
        (s.SnrRatio - 0.1 * ABS(s.NoiseFloorDbm)) AS SNQI,  -- Calculate SNQI
        sd.CompressRatio * sc.ComplexIdx * sc.EntropyVal / 10 AS ECI  -- Calculate ECI
    FROM Signals s
    JOIN SignalProbabilities p ON s.SignalRegistry = p.SignalRef
    JOIN SignalDecoding sd ON s.SignalRegistry = sd.SignalRef
    JOIN SignalClassification sc ON s.SignalRegistry = sc.SignalRef
    WHERE p.TechSigProb > 0.7 
      AND p.NatSrcProb < 0.3 
      AND p.ArtSrcProb < 50
      AND s.BwHz/(s.CenterFreqMhz * 1000000) < 0.001
)
SELECT 
    t.ObservStation,
    COUNT(*) AS potential_tech_signals,
    AVG(ts.SNQI) AS avg_snqi,  -- Replace avg_tols with avg_snqi
    AVG(ts.ECI) AS avg_eci,    -- Replace avg_bfr with avg_eci
    COUNT(*) * 100.0 / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tech_signals) AS percentage_of_total
FROM tech_signals ts
JOIN Telescopes t ON ts.TelescRef = t.TelescRegistry
GROUP BY t.ObservStation
ORDER BY potential_tech_signals DESC;

sol_sql:
/*
Intent: Evaluate effectiveness of different modulation types
Knowledge Used: MCS (id:30)
Advanced Features: JSON functions, advanced aggregation
*/
SELECT 
    s.ModType,
    COUNT(*) AS signal_count,
    AVG(s.ModIndex * (1 + (1 - ABS(s.FreqDriftHzs)/(s.FreqMhz*1000)) * 
        s.SigDurSec/(1 + s.DoppShiftHz/1000)) *
        CASE 
            WHEN s.ModType = 'AM' THEN 2
            WHEN s.ModType = 'FM' THEN 1.5
            ELSE 1
        END) AS avg_mcs,
    AVG(s.SnrRatio) AS avg_snr,
    JSON_OBJECT_AGG(
        s.SignalRegistry,
        JSON_BUILD_OBJECT(
            'mcs', s.ModIndex * (1 + (1 - ABS(s.FreqDriftHzs)/(s.FreqMhz*1000)) * 
                   s.SigDurSec/(1 + s.DoppShiftHz/1000)) *
                   CASE 
                       WHEN s.ModType = 'AM' THEN 2
                       WHEN s.ModType = 'FM' THEN 1.5
                       ELSE 1
                   END,
            'snr', s.SnrRatio
        )
    ) AS signal_details
FROM Signals s
WHERE s.ModType IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY s.ModType
HAVING COUNT(*) > 5;

follow_up.sol_sql:
/*
Intent: Evaluate effectiveness of different modulation types with additional constraint

*/

        CASE 
            WHEN s.ModType = 'AM' THEN 2
            WHEN s.ModType = 'FM' THEN 1.5
            ELSE 1
        END) AS avg_mcs,
    AVG(s.SnrRatio) AS avg_snr,
    JSON_OBJECT_AGG(
        s.SignalRegistry,
        JSON_BUILD_OBJECT(
            'mcs', s.ModIndex * (1 + (1 - ABS(s.FreqDriftHzs)/(s.FreqMhz*1000)) * 
                   s.SigDurSec/(1 + s.DoppShiftHz/1000)) *
                   CASE 
                       WHEN s.ModType = 'AM' THEN 2
                       WHEN s.ModType = 'FM' THEN 1.5

                   END,
            'snr', s.SnrRatio
        )
    ) AS signal_details

sol_sql:
/*
Intent: Optimize research priorities based on multiple factors
Knowledge Used: RPI (id:8), CCS (id:36)
Advanced Features: Complex calculations, window functions
*/
WITH priority_calc AS (
    SELECT 
        s.SignalRegistry,
        (p.TechSigProb * 4 + p.BioSigProb/100 + p.SigUnique * 2 + p.AnomScore/2) * 
            (1 - p.FalsePosProb) AS RPI,
        (1 - p.FalsePosProb) * d.DecodeConf * 
            (CASE WHEN s.SnrRatio - 0.1 * ABS(s.NoiseFloorDbm) > 0 
                  THEN (s.SnrRatio - 0.1 * ABS(s.NoiseFloorDbm))/10 + 0.5 
                  ELSE 0.1 END) AS CCS_approx,
        o.ObservStation
    FROM Signals s
    JOIN SignalProbabilities p ON s.SignalRegistry = p.SignalRef
    JOIN SignalDecoding d ON s.SignalRegistry = d.SignalRef
    JOIN Telescopes t ON s.TelescRef = t.TelescRegistry
    JOIN Observatories o ON t.ObservStation = o.ObservStation
)
SELECT 
    ObservStation,
    COUNT(*) AS signal_count,
    AVG(RPI) AS avg_rpi,
    AVG(CCS_approx) AS avg_ccs,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE RPI > 3) AS high_priority_signals,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE CCS_approx > 0.8) AS high_confidence_signals,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE RPI > 3 AND CCS_approx > 0.8) AS high_priority_high_confidence
FROM priority_calc
GROUP BY ObservStation
ORDER BY high_priority_high_confidence DESC;

follow_up.sol_sql:
/*
Intent: Optimize research priorities with new metrics
Knowledge Used: RPI (id:8), CCS (id:36), MCS (id:30), TOLS (id:3)
Advanced Features: Complex calculations, window functions
*/
WITH priority_calc AS (
    SELECT 
        s.SignalRegistry,
        (p.TechSigProb * 4 + p.BioSigProb/100 + p.SigUnique * 2 + p.AnomScore/2) * 
            (1 - p.FalsePosProb) AS RPI,
        (1 - p.FalsePosProb) * d.DecodeConf * 
            (CASE WHEN s.SnrRatio - 0.1 * ABS(s.NoiseFloorDbm) > 0 
                  THEN (s.SnrRatio - 0.1 * ABS(s.NoiseFloorDbm))/10 + 0.5 
                  ELSE 0.1 END) AS CCS_approx,
        (p.TechSigProb * (1 - p.NatSrcProb) * p.SigUnique * (0.5 + p.AnomScore/10)) AS TOLS,
        (s.ModIndex * (1 + (1 - ABS(s.FreqDriftHzs)/(s.FreqMhz*1000)) * 
            s.SigDurSec/(1 + s.DoppShiftHz/1000)) * 
            CASE 
                WHEN s.ModType = 'AM' THEN 2
                WHEN s.ModType = 'FM' THEN 1.5
                ELSE 1
            END) AS MCS,
        o.ObservStation
    FROM Signals s
    JOIN SignalProbabilities p ON s.SignalRegistry = p.SignalRef
    JOIN SignalDecoding d ON s.SignalRegistry = d.SignalRef
    JOIN Telescopes t ON s.TelescRef = t.TelescRegistry
    JOIN Observatories o ON t.ObservStation = o.ObservStation
)
SELECT 
    ObservStation,
    COUNT(*) AS signal_count,
    AVG(MCS) AS avg_mcs,
    AVG(TOLS) AS avg_tols,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE RPI > 3) AS high_priority_signals,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE CCS_approx > 0.8) AS high_confidence_signals,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE RPI > 3 AND CCS_approx > 0.8) AS high_priority_high_confidence
FROM priority_calc
GROUP BY ObservStation
ORDER BY high_priority_high_confidence DESC;

sol_sql:
-- This query evaluates optimal observatory conditions using Atmospheric Observability Index (AOI)
-- and generates a JSON report of environmental factors using JSON aggregation functions.
-- Using KB knowledge: Atmospheric Observability Index (AOI) [id: 1], Optimal Observing Window (OOW) [id: 13]

-- Step 1: Calculate AOI for each observatory and determine if conditions meet Optimal Observing Window criteria
WITH observatory_conditions AS (
    SELECT 
        o.ObservStation,
        o.AtmosTransparency * (1 - o.HumidityRate/100) * (1 - 0.02 * o.WindSpeedMs) AS aoi,  -- AOI formula from KB
        o.LunarStage,
        o.LunarDistDeg,
        o.SolarStatus,
        -- Check if conditions meet OOW criteria
        CASE WHEN 
            o.AtmosTransparency * (1 - o.HumidityRate/100) * (1 - 0.02 * o.WindSpeedMs) > 0.85 AND
            (o.LunarStage = 'New' OR o.LunarStage = 'First Quarter') AND
            o.LunarDistDeg > 45 AND
            (o.SolarStatus = 'Low' OR o.SolarStatus = 'Moderate')
        THEN TRUE ELSE FALSE END AS is_optimal_window
    FROM 
        Observatories o
)
-- Step 2: Generate JSON report grouping observatories by optimal condition status
SELECT 
    is_optimal_window,
    COUNT(*) AS station_count,
    ROUND(AVG(aoi)::numeric, 3) AS avg_aoi,
    -- Use JSON aggregation to create detailed environmental factors report
    jsonb_agg(jsonb_build_object(
        'station', ObservStation,
        'aoi', ROUND(aoi::numeric, 3),
        'lunar_factors', jsonb_build_object(
            'stage', LunarStage,
            'distance', LunarDistDeg
        ),
        'solar_status', SolarStatus
    )) AS observatory_details
FROM 
    observatory_conditions
GROUP BY 
    is_optimal_window;

follow_up.sol_sql:
-- This query evaluates observatory conditions with modified Optimal Observing Window (OOW) criteria
-- Using KB knowledge: Atmospheric Observability Index (AOI) [id: 1], Optimal Observing Window (OOW) [id: 13], Signal Degradation Scenario (SDS) [id: 14]

-- Step 1: Calculate AOI for each observatory and determine if conditions meet the modified Optimal Observing Window criteria
WITH observatory_conditions AS (
    SELECT 
        o.ObservStation,
        o.AtmosTransparency * (1 - o.HumidityRate/100) * (1 - 0.02 * o.WindSpeedMs) AS aoi,  -- AOI formula from KB
        o.LunarStage,
        o.LunarDistDeg,
        o.SolarStatus,
        -- Modified OOW criteria: relax AOI threshold, add geomagstatus condition, and exclude SDS conditions
        CASE WHEN 
            o.AtmosTransparency * (1 - o.HumidityRate/100) * (1 - 0.02 * o.WindSpeedMs) > 0.75 AND  -- Relaxed AOI threshold from 0.85 to 0.75
            (o.LunarStage = 'New' OR o.LunarStage = 'First Quarter') AND
            o.LunarDistDeg > 45 AND
            (o.SolarStatus = 'Low' OR o.SolarStatus = 'Moderate') AND
            o.GeomagStatus = 'Quiet' AND  -- New condition for geomagnetic status
            NOT (  -- Exclude Signal Degradation Scenario (SDS) conditions
                o.AtmosTransparency < 0.7 OR
                o.HumidityRate > 70 OR
                o.WindSpeedMs > 8 OR
                o.GeomagStatus LIKE '%Storm%'
            )
        THEN TRUE ELSE FALSE END AS is_optimal_window
    FROM 
        Observatories o
)
-- Step 2: Generate JSON report grouping observatories by optimal condition status
SELECT 
    is_optimal_window,
    COUNT(*) AS station_count,
    ROUND(AVG(aoi)::numeric, 3) AS avg_aoi,
    -- Use JSON aggregation to create detailed environmental factors report
    jsonb_agg(jsonb_build_object(
        'station', ObservStation,
        'aoi', ROUND(aoi::numeric, 3),
        'lunar_factors', jsonb_build_object(
            'stage', LunarStage,
            'distance', LunarDistDeg
        ),
        'solar_status', SolarStatus
    )) AS observatory_details
FROM 
    observatory_conditions
GROUP BY 
    is_optimal_window;

sol_sql:
/* 
-- Intent: Find signals matching Narrowband Technological Marker criteria with spectrum stability
-- Uses KB item 15 (NTM) and advanced LATERAL JOIN for computational efficiency
*/
-- Step 1: Calculate BFR using a lateral join to improve query performance
SELECT 
    s.SignalRegistry,
    s.CenterFreqMhz,
    s.FreqDriftHzs,
    -- Calculate BFR using lateral join results
    nbcalc.BFR,
    -- Using CASE for categorization based on NTM criteria
    CASE 
        WHEN nbcalc.BFR < 0.0001 AND s.FreqDriftHzs < 0.1 
        THEN 'Strong NTM'
        WHEN nbcalc.BFR < 0.0005 AND s.FreqDriftHzs < 0.5 
        THEN 'Moderate NTM'
        ELSE 'Not NTM'
    END AS ntm_classification
FROM Signals s
-- Step 2: Use LATERAL JOIN for efficient calculation of BFR
CROSS JOIN LATERAL (
    SELECT (s.BwHz/(s.CenterFreqMhz * 1000000)) AS BFR
) AS nbcalc
-- Step 3: Apply primary NTM criteria from KB item 15
WHERE 
    nbcalc.BFR < 0.001 AND
    s.FreqDriftHzs < 1.0;

follow_up.sol_sql:
/* 
-- Intent: Compare signals matching Narrowband Technological Marker (NTM) profiles with Fast Radio Transients (FRTs)
-- Uses KB item 15 (NTM), KB item 19 (FRT), and KB item 4 (BFR) for signal categorization
*/
-- Step 1: Calculate BFR and classify signals as NTM or FRT
SELECT 
    s.SignalRegistry,
    s.CenterFreqMhz,
    s.FreqDriftHzs,
    nbcalc.BFR,
    -- Classify signal type (NTM or FRT)
    CASE 
        WHEN nbcalc.BFR < 0.001 AND s.FreqDriftHzs < 1.0 THEN 
            CASE 
                WHEN nbcalc.BFR < 0.0001 AND s.FreqDriftHzs < 0.1 THEN 'Strong NTM'
                WHEN nbcalc.BFR < 0.0005 AND s.FreqDriftHzs < 0.5 THEN 'Moderate NTM'
                ELSE 'Not NTM'
            END
        WHEN s.SigDurSec < 0.1 AND s.SigStrDb > 15 AND s.BwHz > 1000000 AND sc.RepeatCount = 1 THEN 'FRT'
 either an NTM category or FRT, otherwise 'Others'
    END AS signal_classification
FROM Signals s
LEFT JOIN SignalClassification sc ON s.SignalRegistry = sc.SignalRef  -- Join to get RepeatCount for FRT criteria
CROSS JOIN LATERAL (
    SELECT (s.BwHz/(s.CenterFreqMhz * 1000000)) AS BFR
) AS nbcalc
-- Step 2: Filter for signals that are either NTM or FRT
WHERE 
    (nbcalc.BFR < 0.001 AND s.FreqDriftHzs < 1.0)  -- NTM criteria
    OR (s.SigDurSec < 0.1 AND s.SigStrDb > 15 AND s.BwHz > 1000000 AND sc.RepeatCount = 1);  -- FRT criteria

sol_sql:
-- Intent: Evaluate combined observatory conditions and equipment status for observation quality
-- Uses KB item 16 (OCL) and 32 (OQF), with advanced array functions
-- Step 1: Calculate observation quality metrics using various observatory and telescope parameters
WITH quality_assessment AS (
    SELECT 
        o.ObservStation,
        t.TelescRegistry,
        o.AtmosTransparency,
        o.HumidityRate,
        o.WindSpeedMs,
        o.LunarDistDeg,
        t.EquipStatus,
        t.CalibrStatus,
        t.PointAccArc,
        -- Calculate AOI from KB item 1
        (o.AtmosTransparency * (1 - o.HumidityRate/100) * (1 - 0.02 * o.WindSpeedMs)) AS AOI,
        -- Calculate LIF from KB item 9
        ((1 - o.LunarDistDeg/180) * (1 - o.AtmosTransparency)) AS LIF,
        -- Store equipment issues in array for analysis
        ARRAY[
            CASE WHEN t.EquipStatus != 'Degraded' THEN t.EquipStatus ELSE NULL END,
            CASE WHEN t.CalibrStatus != 'Overdue' THEN t.CalibrStatus ELSE NULL END,
            CASE WHEN t.CoolSysStatus != 'Warning' THEN t.CoolSysStatus ELSE NULL END
        ] AS equipment_issues
    FROM Observatories o
    INNER JOIN Telescopes t ON o.ObservStation = t.ObservStation
)
-- Step 2: Apply OCL criteria and calculate OQF using conditional logic
SELECT 
    qa.ObservStation,
    qa.TelescRegistry,
    -- Calculate OQF based on KB item 32
    (qa.AOI * (1 - qa.LIF) * (CASE WHEN qa.PointAccArc < 2 THEN 1 ELSE 2/qa.PointAccArc END)) AS OQF,
    -- Display equipment issues using array functions
    ARRAY_TO_STRING(ARRAY_REMOVE(qa.equipment_issues, NULL), ', ') AS identified_issues,
    -- Count equipment issues using array functions
    CARDINALITY(ARRAY_REMOVE(qa.equipment_issues, NULL)) AS issue_count,
    -- Apply OCL classification criteria from KB item 16
    CASE 
        WHEN qa.AOI > 0.8 AND qa.EquipStatus = 'Operational' AND qa.CalibrStatus = 'Current' 
        THEN 'High'
        WHEN qa.AOI BETWEEN 0.5 AND 0.8 
        THEN 'Medium'
        ELSE 'Low'
    END AS ocl_classification
FROM quality_assessment qa
-- Step 3: Order by calculated quality for prioritization
ORDER BY OQF DESC;

follow_up.sol_sql:
/* 
-- Intent: Compare observation quality between observatories with Clear vs. Cloudy weather profiles
-- Uses KB item 16 (OCL), 32 (OQF), and 20 (WeathProfile: Clear) for weather profile context
-- Step 1: Calculate observation quality metrics using various observatory and telescope parameters
*/
WITH quality_assessment AS (
    SELECT 
        o.ObservStation,
        t.TelescRegistry,
        o.WeathProfile,
        o.AtmosTransparency,
        o.HumidityRate,
        o.WindSpeedMs,
        o.LunarDistDeg,
        t.EquipStatus,
        t.CalibrStatus,
        t.PointAccArc,
        -- Calculate AOI from KB item 1
        (o.AtmosTransparency * (1 - o.HumidityRate/100) * (1 - 0.02 * o.WindSpeedMs)) AS AOI,
        -- Calculate LIF from KB item 9
        ((1 - o.LunarDistDeg/180) * (1 - o.AtmosTransparency)) AS LIF,
        -- Store equipment issues in array for analysis
        ARRAY[
            CASE WHEN t.EquipStatus != 'Degraded' THEN t.EquipStatus ELSE NULL END,
            CASE WHEN t.CalibrStatus != 'Overdue' THEN t.CalibrStatus ELSE NULL END,
            CASE WHEN t.CoolSysStatus != 'Warning' THEN t.CoolSysStatus ELSE NULL END
        ] AS equipment_issues
    FROM Observatories o
    INNER JOIN Telescopes t ON o.ObservStation = t.ObservStation
    WHERE o.WeathProfile IN ('Clear', 'Cloudy')  -- Filter for Clear and Cloudy weather profiles
)
-- Step 2: Apply OCL criteria, calculate OQF, and compare by weather profile
SELECT 
    qa.ObservStation,
    qa.TelescRegistry,
    qa.WeathProfile,
    -- Calculate OQF based on KB item 32
    (qa.AOI * (1 - qa.LIF) * (CASE WHEN qa.PointAccArc < 2 THEN 1 ELSE 2/qa.PointAccArc END)) AS OQF,
    -- Display equipment issues using array functions
    ARRAY_TO_STRING(ARRAY_REMOVE(qa.equipment_issues, NULL), ', ') AS identified_issues,
    -- Count equipment issues using array functions
    CARDINALITY(ARRAY_REMOVE(qa.equipment_issues, NULL)) AS issue_count,
    -- Apply OCL classification criteria from KB item 16
    CASE 
        WHEN qa.AOI > 0.8 AND qa.EquipStatus = 'Operational' AND qa.CalibrStatus = 'Current' 
        THEN 'High'
        WHEN qa.AOI BETWEEN 0.5 AND 0.8 
        THEN 'Medium'
        ELSE 'Low'
    END AS ocl_classification
FROM quality_assessment qa
-- Step 3: Order by weather profile and then by OQF for comparison
ORDER BY qa.WeathProfile, OQF DESC;

-- Intent: Identify signals showing evidence of coherent information patterns based on stability
-- Uses KB item 7 (SSM) and 11 (CIP), with advanced string aggregation
-- Step 1: Calculate Signal Stability Metric (SSM) using mathematical operations
WITH stability_analysis AS (
    SELECT 
        s.SignalRegistry,
        s.SignalClass,
        s.FreqDriftHzs,
        s.FreqMhz,
        s.SigDurSec,
        s.DoppShiftHz,
        s.ModType,
        s.ModIndex,
        sd.SigIntegrity,
        sd.FreqStab,
        sd.PhaseStab,
        sc.EntropyVal,
        -- Calculate SSM per KB item 7
        ((1 - ABS(s.FreqDriftHzs)/(s.FreqMhz * 1000)) * 
         (s.SigDurSec/(1 + s.DoppShiftHz/1000))) AS SSM
    FROM Signals s
    INNER JOIN SignalDynamics sd ON s.SignalRegistry = sd.SignalRef
    INNER JOIN SignalClassification sc ON s.SignalRegistry = sc.SignalRef
)
-- Step 2: Analyze coherent information patterns with string aggregation
SELECT 
    sa.SignalRegistry,
    sa.SignalClass,
    ROUND(sa.SSM::numeric, 3) AS SSM,
    -- Aggregate stability factors using string_agg
    string_agg(
        CASE 
            WHEN attribute = 'FreqStab' THEN 'Frequency: ' || value
            WHEN attribute = 'PhaseStab' THEN 'Phase: ' || value
            WHEN attribute = 'SigIntegrity' THEN 'Integrity: ' || value
        END,
        ', ' ORDER BY attribute
    ) AS stability_factors,
    -- Evaluate if signal meets CIP criteria from KB item 11
    CASE 
        WHEN sa.SSM > 0.8 AND 
             sa.EntropyVal BETWEEN 0.4 AND 0.8 AND
             sa.ModIndex > 0.5
        THEN 'Coherent Information Pattern Detected'
        WHEN sa.SSM > 0.6 AND sa.EntropyVal BETWEEN 0.3 AND 0.9
        THEN 'Potential Information Pattern'
        ELSE 'No Clear Pattern'
    END AS pattern_assessment
FROM stability_analysis sa
-- Step 3: Use lateral join with unnest to transform stability attributes into rows for aggregation
CROSS JOIN LATERAL (
    SELECT unnest(ARRAY['FreqStab', 'PhaseStab', 'SigIntegrity']) AS attribute,
           unnest(ARRAY[sa.FreqStab, sa.PhaseStab, sa.SigIntegrity]) AS value
) AS stability_attrs
GROUP BY 
    sa.SignalRegistry, sa.SignalClass, sa.SSM, sa.EntropyVal, sa.ModType, sa.ModIndex;

-- Create a function to calculate Detection Instrument Sensitivity Factor (DISF).
-- Intent: Define a function that computes DISF using telescope environmental data and then test it.
-- Step 1: Create the function "calculate_disf" using PL/pgSQL.
--         Inputs: AirTempC, AtmosTransparency, HumidityRate, LunarDistDeg.
--         Calculation: DISF = (10 - |AirTempC - 15|/10) * AtmosTransparency * (1 - HumidityRate/200) * ((100 - LunarDistDeg)/100)
-- Knowledge Used:
--   "Detection Instrument Sensitivity Factor (DISF)" [KB id:5] and advanced procedural language features.
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION calculate_disf(
    p_airtemp NUMERIC, 
    p_trans NUMERIC, 
    p_humidity NUMERIC, 
    p_lunar_deg NUMERIC
) RETURNS NUMERIC AS $$
DECLARE
    v_disf NUMERIC;
BEGIN
    -- Calculate the DISF based on the given formula.
    v_disf := (10 - ABS(p_airtemp - 15)/10) * p_trans * (1 - p_humidity/200) * ((100 - p_lunar_deg)/100);
    RETURN v_disf;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
test_cases:
def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):
    # The expected DISF value computed for inputs: AirTempC = 20, p_trans = 0.95, p_humidity = 45, p_lunar_deg = 60.
    # Calculation:
    #   factor1 = 10 - ABS(20 - 15)/10 = 10 - 5/10 = 10 - 0.5 = 9.5
    #   factor2 = 0.95
    #   factor3 = 1 - 45/200 = 1 - 0.225 = 0.775
    #   factor4 = (100 - 60)/100 = 40/100 = 0.4
    # Expected DISF = 9.5 * 0.95 * 0.775 * 0.4 ≈ 2.79775
    expected_disf = 2.79775
    tolerance = 0.00001  # Allowable floating-point error
    
    # Execute a SELECT to call calculate_disf with given parameters.
    pred_result, _, _ = execute_queries("SELECT calculate_disf(20, 0.95, 45, 60);", db_name, conn)
    # Expecting pred_result to be a list with one tuple containing the computed value.
    predicted_value = float(pred_result[0][0])
    
    # Step 3: Assert that the predicted value matches the expected DISF.
    assert abs(predicted_value - expected_disf) < tolerance, (
        f"Predicted DISF value {predicted_value} differs from expected {expected_disf}"
    )

-- Create a view to show observation quality factors.
-- Intent: Join Observatories, Telescopes, and Signals to compute an "Observation Quality Factor (OQF)"
--         that is based on AOI, LIF, and pointing accuracy.
--
-- Step 1: Use a CTE to join three tables using INNER JOIN.
-- Step 2: Compute AOI = AtmosTransparency * (1 - HumidityRate/100) * (1 - 0.02 * WindSpeedMs).
-- Step 3: Compute LIF = (1 - LunarDistDeg/180) * (1 - AtmosTransparency).
-- Step 4: Compute OQF = AOI * (1 - LIF) * (CASE WHEN PointAccArc < 2 THEN 1 ELSE 2/PointAccArc END).
--
-- Advanced features used: CASE expressions, arithmetic operations, and a CTE for cleaner joins.
--
-- Knowledge Used:
--   "Atmospheric Observability Index (AOI)" [KB id:1],
--   "Lunar Interference Factor (LIF)" [KB id:9],
--   and "Observation Quality Factor (OQF)" [KB id:32].
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW vw_observation_quality AS
WITH calc AS (
  SELECT
    o.ObservStation,
    t.TelescRegistry,
    s.SignalRegistry,
    o.AtmosTransparency,
    o.HumidityRate,
    o.WindSpeedMs,
    o.LunarDistDeg,
    t.PointAccArc,
    -- Compute AOI based on environmental conditions.
    (o.AtmosTransparency * (1 - o.HumidityRate/100.0) * (1 - 0.02 * o.WindSpeedMs)) AS aoi,
    -- Compute LIF based on lunar distance and transparency.
    ((1 - o.LunarDistDeg/180.0) * (1 - o.AtmosTransparency)) AS lif
  FROM Observatories o
  INNER JOIN Telescopes t ON o.ObservStation = t.ObservStation
  INNER JOIN Signals s ON s.TelescRef = t.TelescRegistry
)
SELECT
  ObservStation,
  TelescRegistry,
  SignalRegistry,
  aoi,
  lif,
  aoi * (1 - lif) * (CASE WHEN PointAccArc < 2 THEN 1 ELSE 2.0/PointAccArc END) AS oqf
FROM calc;
test_cases:
def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):
    tolerance = 0.0001  # Allowable floating point difference
    # Define a verification query that joins the view with base tables to compute expected oqf.
    verification_sql = """
    SELECT 
        v.oqf AS view_oqf,
        (calc.aoi * (1 - calc.lif) * 
            (CASE WHEN calc.PointAccArc < 2 THEN 1 ELSE 2.0/calc.PointAccArc END)
        ) AS expected_oqf
    FROM vw_observation_quality v
    JOIN (
        SELECT
            o.ObservStation,
            t.TelescRegistry,
            s.SignalRegistry,
            (o.AtmosTransparency * (1 - o.HumidityRate/100.0) * (1 - 0.02 * o.WindSpeedMs)) AS aoi,
            ((1 - o.LunarDistDeg/180.0) * (1 - o.AtmosTransparency)) AS lif,
            t.PointAccArc
        FROM Observatories o
        INNER JOIN Telescopes t ON o.ObservStation = t.ObservStation
        INNER JOIN Signals s ON s.TelescRef = t.TelescRegistry
    ) calc
    ON v.ObservStation = calc.ObservStation
    AND v.TelescRegistry = calc.TelescRegistry
    AND v.SignalRegistry = calc.SignalRegistry;
    """
    # Execute verification query.
    pred_verification_result, _, _ = execute_queries(verification_sql, db_name, conn)
    # For each returned row, assert that view_oqf is within tolerance of expected_oqf.
    for row in pred_verification_result:
        view_oqf = float(row[0])
        expected_oqf = float(row[1])
        assert abs(view_oqf - expected_oqf) < tolerance, (
            f"Predicted view error: For row with ObservStation/TelescRegistry/SignalRegistry, "
            f"view oqf ({view_oqf}) does not match expected oqf ({expected_oqf}) within tolerance."
        )

CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW vw_high_quality_observations AS
WITH calc AS (
  SELECT
    o.ObservStation,
    t.TelescRegistry,
    s.SignalRegistry,
    o.AtmosTransparency,
    o.HumidityRate,
    o.WindSpeedMs,
    o.LunarDistDeg,
    t.PointAccArc,
    (o.AtmosTransparency * (1 - o.HumidityRate/100.0) * (1 - 0.02 * o.WindSpeedMs)) AS aoi,
    ((1 - o.LunarDistDeg/180.0) * (1 - o.AtmosTransparency)) AS lif
  FROM Observatories o
  INNER JOIN Telescopes t ON o.ObservStation = t.ObservStation
  INNER JOIN Signals s ON s.TelescRef = t.TelescRegistry
  WHERE (o.AtmosTransparency * (1 - o.HumidityRate/100.0) * (1 - 0.02 * o.WindSpeedMs)) > 0.8
    AND ((1 - o.LunarDistDeg/180.0) * (1 - o.AtmosTransparency)) < 0.3
)
SELECT
  ObservStation,
  TelescRegistry,
  SignalRegistry,
  aoi,
  lif,
  aoi * (1 - lif) * (CASE WHEN PointAccArc < 2 THEN 1 ELSE 2.0/PointAccArc END) AS oqf
FROM calc;

-- Update telescope equipment status based on Lunar Interference Factor (LIF).
-- Step 1: Dynamically compute LIF using:
--         LIF = (1 - LunarDistDeg/180) × (1 - AtmosTransparency).
--         Values above 0.5 suggest significant lunar contamination.
-- Step 2: Update telescope equipment status to 'Under Lunar Interference' for high LIF values.
-- Step 3: Use a subquery to JOIN `Telescopes` with `Observatories` for the necessary data.

-- Knowledge Used:
--   - "Lunar Interference Factor (LIF)" [KB id:9]
--   - Advanced SQL: Subqueries with JOIN and conditional updates.

UPDATE Telescopes
SET EquipStatus = 'Under Lunar Interference'
WHERE EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM Observatories o
    WHERE o.ObservStation = Telescopes.ObservStation
      AND (1 - o.LunarDistDeg / 180.0) * (1 - o.AtmosTransparency) > 0.5
);
-- Result: Updates telescopes where lunar interference is deemed significant.

    AND t.EquipStatus <> 'Under Lunar Interference';
    """

    pred_result, _, _ = execute_queries(verification_sql, db_name, conn)

/*
 * SQL Intent: Calculate the average Scan Quality Score (SQS) for each site to evaluate scan quality.
 * Knowledge Used: #3 (Scan Quality Score - SQS)
 * Advanced Functions: LOG(), POWER(), AVG(), GROUP BY
 */

SELECT
    s.zoneref AS site_code,
    si.zonelabel AS site_name,
    -- Step 1: Calculate the average SQS for each site and round to 2 decimal places
    ROUND(
        AVG(
            POWER(10.0 / (LOG(10, spc.scanresolmm * 1000.0) / LOG(10, spc.pointdense)) * 5.0, 1.5) *
            (spc.coverpct / 100.0) *
            POWER(1.0 - spc.noisedb / 30.0, 2)
        ),
        2
    ) AS avg_sqs
FROM scans s
JOIN scanpointcloud spc ON s.arcref = spc.arcref
JOIN sites si ON s.zoneref = si.zoneregistry
GROUP BY s.zoneref, si.zonelabel
ORDER BY avg_sqs DESC;

follow_up.sol_sql:
/* -----------------------------------------------------------------
 * Add the Environmental Suitability Index (ESI) and classify it with
 * the Environmental Condition Classification System (ECCS) tier
 * for every site, while keeping the previous average SQS ranking.
 * Knowledge used:
 *   #3  – Scan Quality Score (SQS)
 *   #7  – Environmental Suitability Index (ESI)
 *   #50 – Environmental Condition Classification System (ECCS)
 * -----------------------------------------------------------------*/

WITH sqs_per_scan AS (          -- ① SQS for every scan/point-cloud pair
    SELECT
        s.zoneref,
        POWER(
            10.0 /                                  -- 10 / SRI
            ( LOG(10, spc.scanresolmm * 1000.0)
              / LOG(10, spc.pointdense) )            -- SRI denominator
            * 5.0,                                   -- ×5  (see SRI def.)
            1.5                                      -- ^1.5  (SQS weight)
        )
        * (spc.coverpct / 100.0)                     -- SCE term
        * POWER(1.0 - spc.noisedb / 30.0, 2)         -- Noise term
        AS sqs
    FROM scans            s
    JOIN scanpointcloud   spc ON s.arcref = spc.arcref
),
avg_sqs_site AS (            -- ② average SQS per site
    SELECT
        zoneref,
        ROUND(AVG(sqs), 2) AS avg_sqs
    FROM   sqs_per_scan
    GROUP  BY zoneref
),
esi_per_rec AS (             -- ③ ESI for every environment record
    SELECT
        zoneref,
        100
        - 2.5 * ABS(ambictemp - 20)                       -- temperature term
        - POWER(ABS((humepct - 50) / 2), 1.5)             -- humidity term
        - 600.0 / (illumelux + 100)                       -- illumination term
        AS esi
    FROM scanenvironment
),
avg_esi_site AS (            -- ④ average ESI per site
    SELECT
        zoneref,
        ROUND(AVG(esi), 2) AS avg_esi
    FROM   esi_per_rec
    GROUP  BY zoneref
),
site_scores AS (             -- ⑤ merge quality and environment metrics
    SELECT
        s.zoneref,
        s.avg_sqs,
        e.avg_esi
    FROM   avg_sqs_site s
    LEFT   JOIN avg_esi_site e
           ON s.zoneref = e.zoneref
)
SELECT
    ss.zoneref        AS site_code,
    si.zonelabel      AS site_name,
    ss.avg_sqs,
    ss.avg_esi,
    CASE                               /* ECCS tier (#50) */
        WHEN ss.avg_esi >  85 THEN 'Optimal Scanning Conditions'
        WHEN ss.avg_esi >= 70 THEN 'Good Scanning Conditions'
        WHEN ss.avg_esi >= 50 THEN 'Acceptable Scanning Conditions'
        ELSE                           'Challenging Scanning Conditions'
    END            AS environment_tier
FROM   site_scores ss
JOIN   sites       si ON ss.zoneref = si.zoneregistry
ORDER  BY ss.avg_sqs DESC;             -- keep ranking by scan quality

/*
 * SQL Intent: Identify sites in "Degradation Risk Zones" based on preservation status and structural stability.
 * Knowledge Used: #14 (Degradation Risk Zone), #26, #52
 * Advanced Functions: CASE, String Matching (IN)
 */

SELECT
    si.zoneregistry AS site_code,
    si.zonelabel AS site_name,
    sc.structstate AS structural_state,
    si.presstat AS preservation_status,
    -- Step 1: Classify sites into degradation risk zones
    CASE
        WHEN si.presstat IN ('Poor', 'Critical') AND sc.structstate <> 'Stable' THEN 'Degradation Risk Zone'
        ELSE 'Not in Risk Zone'
    END AS risk_zone
FROM sites si
LEFT JOIN scanconservation sc ON si.zoneregistry = sc.zoneref;

follow_up.sol_sql:
/* Aggregate the sites by their Risk Zone Category
 * Knowledge used: #14 (Degradation Risk Zone), #26, #52
 */
SELECT
    CASE
        WHEN si.presstat IN ('Poor', 'Critical')
             AND sc.structstate <> 'Stable'
        THEN 'Degradation Risk Zone'
        ELSE 'Not in Risk Zone'
    END AS risk_zone,
    COUNT(*) AS site_count
FROM sites            si
LEFT JOIN scanconservation sc
       ON sc.zoneref = si.zoneregistry
GROUP BY risk_zone
ORDER BY site_count DESC;

/*
 * SQL Intent: Calculate the Environmental Suitability Index (ESI) for each site based on temperature, humidity, and light conditions.
 * Knowledge Used: #7 (Environmental Suitability Index - ESI)
 * Advanced Functions: ABS(), POWER(), AVG(), GROUP BY
 */

SELECT
    se.zoneref AS site_code,
    si.zonelabel AS site_name,
    -- Step 1: Calculate the average ESI for each site and round to 2 decimal places
    ROUND(
        AVG(
            100.0 - 2.5 * ABS(se.ambictemp - 20.0) -
            POWER(ABS((se.humepct - 50.0) / 2.0), 1.5) -
            600.0 / (se.illumelux + 100.0)
        ),
        2
    ) AS avg_esi
FROM scanenvironment se
JOIN sites si ON se.zoneref = si.zoneregistry
GROUP BY se.zoneref, si.zonelabel;

follow_up.sol_sql:
WITH site_esi AS (
    SELECT
        se.zoneref  AS site_code,
        si.zonelabel AS site_name,
        AVG(
              100.0
            - 2.5 * ABS(se.ambictemp - 20.0)
            - POWER(ABS((se.humepct - 50.0) / 2.0), 1.5)
            - 600.0 / (se.illumelux + 100.0)
        ) AS avg_esi
    FROM scanenvironment se
    JOIN sites          si ON se.zoneref = si.zoneregistry
    GROUP BY se.zoneref, si.zonelabel
)
/* Categorise each site, then aggregate */
SELECT
    CASE
        WHEN avg_esi >  85                       THEN 'Optimal Scanning Conditions'
        WHEN avg_esi >= 70 AND avg_esi <= 85     THEN 'Good Scanning Conditions'
        WHEN avg_esi >= 50 AND avg_esi <  70     THEN 'Acceptable Scanning Conditions'
        ELSE                                          'Challenging Scanning Conditions'
    END   AS eccs_category,
    COUNT(*) AS site_count
FROM site_esi
GROUP BY eccs_category
ORDER BY site_count DESC;

sol_sql:
/*
 * SQL Intent: Analyze registration accuracy and classify confidence levels based on RAR and reference markers.
 * Knowledge Used: #33 (Registration Accuracy Ratio - RAR), #44 (Registration Confidence Level)
 * Advanced Functions: SQRT(), CASE, Window Function (RANK())
 */

SELECT
    sr.logregistry AS registration_id,
    sr.arcref AS project_id,
    sr.logaccumm AS registration_accuracy_mm,
    sr.errvalmm AS error_value_mm,
    -- Step 1: Calculate RAR and round to 2 decimal places
    ROUND(
        (spc.scanresolmm / (sr.logaccumm * SQRT(1.0 + sr.errvalmm / sr.logaccumm))),
        2
    ) AS rar,
    sr.logmethod,
    -- Step 2: Classify confidence level
    CASE
        WHEN (spc.scanresolmm / (sr.logaccumm * SQRT(1.0 + sr.errvalmm / sr.logaccumm))) > 1.5
             AND sr.refmark LIKE '%Target%' THEN 'High Confidence'
        WHEN (spc.scanresolmm / (sr.logaccumm * SQRT(1.0 + sr.errvalmm / sr.logaccumm))) BETWEEN 1.0 AND 1.5 THEN 'Medium Confidence'
        ELSE 'Low Confidence'
    END AS confidence_level
FROM scanregistration sr
JOIN scanpointcloud spc ON sr.arcref = spc.arcref;

follow_up.sol_sql:
/* -----------------------------------------------------------------
 * Filter the previous registration-quality list to keep only those
 * that satisfy the “Registration Quality Threshold” (KB #18), i.e.
 * LogAccuMm < 1 mm  AND  ErrValMm < 2 mm.
 * Recalculate RAR, classify confidence, and flag the threshold pass.
 * ----------------------------------------------------------------- */
WITH rar_data AS (
    SELECT
        sr.logregistry            AS registration_id,
        sr.arcref                 AS project_id,
        sr.logaccumm              AS registration_accuracy_mm,
        sr.errvalmm               AS error_value_mm,
        sr.logmethod,
        spc.scanresolmm,
        (spc.scanresolmm /
         (sr.logaccumm * SQRT(1.0 + sr.errvalmm / sr.logaccumm))
        )                         AS rar_raw
    FROM   scanregistration sr
    JOIN   scanpointcloud  spc  ON spc.arcref = sr.arcref
)
SELECT
    registration_id,
    project_id,
    registration_accuracy_mm,
    error_value_mm,
    ROUND(rar_raw, 2)                           AS rar,
    CASE
        WHEN rar_raw > 1.5
         AND logmethod ILIKE '%Target%'         THEN 'High Confidence'
        WHEN rar_raw BETWEEN 1.0 AND 1.5        THEN 'Medium Confidence'
        ELSE                                         'Low Confidence'
    END                                         AS confidence_level
FROM   rar_data
WHERE  registration_accuracy_mm < 1.0      -- KB #18
  AND  error_value_mm          < 2.0        -- KB #18
ORDER  BY rar DESC;

sol_sql:
/*
 * SQL Intent: Evaluate the Digital Preservation Quality (DPQ) for each site based on ADC, MFS, RAR, and SCE.
 * Knowledge Used: #9 (ADC), #6 (MFS), #33 (RAR), #1 (SCE), #38 (DPQ)
 * Advanced Functions: SQRT(), ROUND(), Aggregation, JOIN
 */

SELECT
    si.zoneregistry AS site_code,
    si.zonelabel AS site_name,
    -- Step 1: Calculate DPQ based on weighted components and round to 2 decimal places
    ROUND(
        CAST(
            (0.3 * COALESCE(adc.adc, 0)) +
            (0.3 * COALESCE(mfs.mfs, 0)) +
            (0.2 * COALESCE(rar.rar, 0)) +
            (0.2 * COALESCE(sce.sce, 0)) -
            (2 * SQRT(COALESCE(err.error_ratio, 0)))
        AS NUMERIC), 
        2
    ) AS dpq
FROM sites si
LEFT JOIN (
    -- Step 2: Calculate Archaeological Documentation Completeness (ADC)
    SELECT
        s.zoneref,
        AVG(
            (sqs.sqs * 0.4) + 
            (sm.mfs * 0.4) + 
            (spc.coverpct * (1.0 + spc.lappct / 100.0 * (1.0 - spc.coverpct / 100.0)) * 0.2) - 
            (5 * SQRT(spc.noisedb / 10.0))
        ) AS adc
    FROM scans s
    JOIN scanpointcloud spc ON s.arcref = spc.arcref
    LEFT JOIN (
        -- SQS calculation
        SELECT 
            sc.arcref,
            AVG(POWER(10.0 / (LOG(10, sc.scanresolmm * 1000.0) / LOG(10, sc.pointdense)) * 5.0, 1.5) *
                (sc.coverpct * (1.0 + sc.lappct / 100.0 * (1.0 - sc.coverpct / 100.0)) / 100.0) *
                POWER(1.0 - sc.noisedb / 30.0, 2)) AS sqs
        FROM scanpointcloud sc
        GROUP BY sc.arcref
    ) sqs ON s.arcref = sqs.arcref
    LEFT JOIN (
        -- MFS calculation
        SELECT 
            sm.zoneref,
            AVG((sm.facetfaces / (sm.facetverts * sm.facetresmm * sm.facetresmm) * 1000.0) * 
                (sm.texpix / (SQRT(sm.facetfaces) * sm.facetresmm) * 0.01 / 10.0) * 
                (1.0 + EXP(-sm.geomdeltamm))) AS mfs
        FROM scanmesh sm
        WHERE sm.facetverts > 0 AND sm.facetfaces > 0 AND sm.facetresmm > 0
        GROUP BY sm.zoneref
    ) sm ON s.zoneref = sm.zoneref
    GROUP BY s.zoneref
) adc ON si.zoneregistry = adc.zoneref
LEFT JOIN (
    -- Step 3: Calculate Model Fidelity Score (MFS)
    SELECT
        sm.zoneref,
        AVG((sm.facetfaces / (sm.facetverts * sm.facetresmm * sm.facetresmm) * 1000.0) * 
            (sm.texpix / (SQRT(sm.facetfaces) * sm.facetresmm) * 0.01 / 10.0) * 
            (1.0 + EXP(-sm.geomdeltamm))) AS mfs
    FROM scanmesh sm
    WHERE sm.facetverts > 0 AND sm.facetfaces > 0 AND sm.facetresmm > 0
    GROUP BY sm.zoneref
) mfs ON si.zoneregistry = mfs.zoneref
LEFT JOIN (
    -- Step 4: Calculate Registration Accuracy Ratio (RAR)
    SELECT
        s.zoneref,
        AVG(spc.scanresolmm / (sr.logaccumm * SQRT(1.0 + sr.errvalmm / sr.logaccumm))) AS rar
    FROM scanregistration sr
    JOIN scanpointcloud spc ON sr.arcref = spc.arcref
    JOIN scans s ON sr.arcref = s.arcref
    WHERE sr.logaccumm > 0
    GROUP BY s.zoneref
) rar ON si.zoneregistry = rar.zoneref
LEFT JOIN (
    -- Step 5: Calculate Scan Coverage Effectiveness (SCE)
    SELECT
        s.zoneref,
        AVG(spc.coverpct * (1.0 + spc.lappct / 100.0 * (1.0 - spc.coverpct / 100.0))) AS sce
    FROM scans s
    JOIN scanpointcloud spc ON s.arcref = spc.arcref
    GROUP BY s.zoneref
) sce ON si.zoneregistry = sce.zoneref
LEFT JOIN (
    -- Step 6: Calculate Error Ratio (ErrValMm/ScanResolMm)
    SELECT
        s.zoneref,
        AVG(sr.errvalmm / spc.scanresolmm) AS error_ratio
    FROM scanregistration sr
    JOIN scanpointcloud spc ON sr.arcref = spc.arcref
    JOIN scans s ON sr.arcref = s.arcref
    WHERE spc.scanresolmm > 0
    GROUP BY s.zoneref
) err ON si.zoneregistry = err.zoneref
ORDER BY dpq DESC;

follow_up.sol_sql:
//*  Degradation Risk Zone filtering added to the original DPQ ranking.
 *  New knowledge used: #14 (Degradation Risk Zone) and #26 (StructState values)
 */
WITH dpq_scores AS (          -- identical computation from the first round
    SELECT
        si.zoneregistry  AS site_code,
        si.zonelabel     AS site_name,
        ROUND(
            ( 0.3 * COALESCE(adc.adc,0)
            + 0.3 * COALESCE(mfs.mfs,0)
            + 0.2 * COALESCE(rar.rar,0)
            + 0.2 * COALESCE(sce.sce,0)
            - 2  * SQRT(COALESCE(err.error_ratio,0))
            )::NUMERIC , 2
        )                AS dpq
    FROM   sites si
    /* ---------- identical LEFT JOIN blocks for adc, mfs, rar, sce, err ---------- */
    LEFT JOIN (                            -- ADC
        SELECT
            s.zoneref,
            AVG(
                  (sqs.sqs * 0.4)
                + (sm.mfs * 0.4)
                + (spc.coverpct * (1 + spc.lappct/100*(1-spc.coverpct/100)) * 0.2)
                - (5 * SQRT(spc.noisedb/10.0))
            ) AS adc
        FROM   scans            s
        JOIN   scanpointcloud   spc ON s.arcref = spc.arcref
        LEFT  JOIN (            -- SQS
            SELECT  sc.arcref,
                    AVG( POWER( 10.0 /
                                 (LOG(10,sc.scanresolmm*1000) / LOG(10,sc.pointdense))*5 , 1.5)
                       * (sc.coverpct*(1+sc.lappct/100*(1-sc.coverpct/100))/100)
                       * POWER(1-sc.noisedb/30,2) ) AS sqs
            FROM   scanpointcloud sc
            GROUP  BY sc.arcref
        ) sqs ON sqs.arcref = s.arcref
        LEFT  JOIN (            -- MFS for ADC part
            SELECT sm.zoneref,
                   AVG(
                       (sm.facetfaces/(sm.facetverts*sm.facetresmm^2)*1000)
                     * (sm.texpix/(SQRT(sm.facetfaces)*sm.facetresmm)*0.01/10)
                     * (1+EXP(-sm.geomdeltamm))
                   ) AS mfs
            FROM   scanmesh sm
            WHERE  sm.facetverts>0 AND sm.facetfaces>0 AND sm.facetresmm>0
            GROUP  BY sm.zoneref
        ) sm  ON sm.zoneref = s.zoneref
        GROUP BY s.zoneref
    ) adc ON adc.zoneref = si.zoneregistry

    LEFT JOIN (                 -- stand-alone MFS
        SELECT sm.zoneref,
               AVG(
                   (sm.facetfaces/(sm.facetverts*sm.facetresmm^2)*1000)
                 * (sm.texpix/(SQRT(sm.facetfaces)*sm.facetresmm)*0.01/10)
                 * (1+EXP(-sm.geomdeltamm))
               ) AS mfs
        FROM   scanmesh sm
        WHERE  sm.facetverts>0 AND sm.facetfaces>0 AND sm.facetresmm>0
        GROUP  BY sm.zoneref
    ) mfs ON mfs.zoneref = si.zoneregistry

    LEFT JOIN (                 -- RAR
        SELECT s.zoneref,
               AVG( spc.scanresolmm /
                    (sr.logaccumm * SQRT(1+sr.errvalmm/sr.logaccumm))
               ) AS rar
        FROM   scanregistration sr
        JOIN   scans           s   ON s.arcref  = sr.arcref
        JOIN   scanpointcloud  spc ON spc.arcref = sr.arcref
        WHERE  sr.logaccumm > 0
        GROUP  BY s.zoneref
    ) rar ON rar.zoneref = si.zoneregistry

    LEFT JOIN (                 -- SCE
        SELECT s.zoneref,
               AVG( spc.coverpct *
                    (1 + spc.lappct/100*(1-spc.coverpct/100))
               ) AS sce
        FROM   scans s
        JOIN   scanpointcloud spc ON spc.arcref = s.arcref
        GROUP  BY s.zoneref
    ) sce ON sce.zoneref = si.zoneregistry

    LEFT JOIN (                 -- Error ratio  (ErrValMm / ScanResolMm)
        SELECT s.zoneref,
               AVG( sr.errvalmm / spc.scanresolmm ) AS error_ratio
        FROM   scanregistration sr
        JOIN   scans           s   ON s.arcref  = sr.arcref
        JOIN   scanpointcloud  spc ON spc.arcref = sr.arcref
        WHERE  spc.scanresolmm > 0
        GROUP  BY s.zoneref
    ) err ON err.zoneref = si.zoneregistry
)
SELECT
    d.site_code,
    d.site_name,
    d.dpq
FROM   dpq_scores      d
JOIN   sites           s  ON s.zoneregistry = d.site_code
WHERE  s.presstat IN ('Poor','Critical')                 -- Degradation Risk Zone (part 1)
  AND  EXISTS (                                          -- Degradation Risk Zone (part 2)
        SELECT 1
        FROM   scanconservation sc
        WHERE  sc.zoneref = s.zoneregistry
          AND  (sc.structstate IS NULL OR sc.structstate <> 'Stable')
      )
ORDER  BY d.dpq DESC;

sol_sql:
/*
 * SQL Intent: Identify sites with High Fidelity Meshes and analyze mesh quality distribution
 * Knowledge Used: #13 (High Fidelity Mesh), #4 (Mesh Complexity Ratio - MCR) #53
 * Advanced Functions: CASE expressions, Window functions (RANK()), String functions
 */

WITH mesh_analysis AS (
    -- Step 1: Calculate Mesh Complexity Ratio (MCR) and identify High Fidelity Meshes
    SELECT 
        sm.zoneref AS site_code,
        sm.facetregistry AS mesh_id,
        -- Calculate MCR based on Knowledge #4
        (sm.facetfaces / (sm.facetverts * POWER(sm.facetresmm, 2))) * 1000 AS mcr,
        sm.facetresmm,
        sm.geomdeltamm,
        -- Determine if mesh is high-fidelity based on Knowledge #13
        CASE 
            WHEN (sm.facetfaces / (sm.facetverts * POWER(sm.facetresmm, 2))) * 1000 > 5.0 
                 AND sm.facetresmm < 1.0 
                 AND sm.geomdeltamm < 0.5 THEN TRUE
            ELSE FALSE
        END AS is_high_fidelity
    FROM scanmesh sm
    WHERE sm.facetverts > 0 AND sm.facetfaces > 0 -- Avoid division by zero
)

-- Step 2: Aggregate mesh quality metrics by site and identify sites with high-fidelity meshes
SELECT 
    s.zoneregistry AS site_code,
    s.zonelabel AS site_name,
    COUNT(ma.mesh_id) AS total_meshes,
    -- Count high-fidelity meshes
    SUM(CASE WHEN ma.is_high_fidelity THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS high_fidelity_count,
    -- Calculate percentage of high-fidelity meshes
    ROUND((SUM(CASE WHEN ma.is_high_fidelity THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::numeric / 
           NULLIF(COUNT(ma.mesh_id), 0) * 100), 2) AS high_fidelity_percentage,
    -- Calculate average MCR, resolution, and geometric accuracy
    ROUND(AVG(ma.mcr)::numeric, 2) AS avg_mcr,
    ROUND(AVG(ma.facetresmm)::numeric, 2) AS avg_mesh_resolution_mm,
    ROUND(AVG(ma.geomdeltamm)::numeric, 2) AS avg_geometric_accuracy_mm,
    -- Determine site's mesh quality classification
    CASE 
        WHEN COUNT(ma.mesh_id) = 0 THEN 'No Mesh Data'
        WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN ma.is_high_fidelity THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0 THEN 'Has High-Fidelity Meshes'
        ELSE 'Standard Mesh Quality'
    END AS mesh_quality_classification
FROM sites s
LEFT JOIN mesh_analysis ma ON s.zoneregistry = ma.site_code
GROUP BY s.zoneregistry, s.zonelabel
ORDER BY high_fidelity_percentage DESC, high_fidelity_count DESC;

follow_up.sol_sql:
/*  Pivot the previous mesh-quality analysis from “site” to “equipment type”
 *  Knowledge reused: #4 (MCR) and #13 (High-Fidelity Mesh)
 */
WITH mesh_analysis AS (          -- compute per-mesh metrics & HF flag
    SELECT
        sm.equipref,
        e.equipform            AS equipment_type,
        (sm.facetfaces / (sm.facetverts * POWER(sm.facetresmm , 2))) * 1000  AS mcr,
        sm.facetresmm,
        sm.geomdeltamm,
        CASE
            WHEN (sm.facetfaces / (sm.facetverts * POWER(sm.facetresmm , 2))) * 1000 > 5.0
                 AND sm.facetresmm < 1.0
                 AND sm.geomdeltamm < 0.5
            THEN TRUE
            ELSE FALSE
        END AS is_high_fidelity
    FROM scanmesh     sm
    JOIN equipment    e  ON e.equipregistry = sm.equipref
    WHERE sm.facetverts > 0
      AND sm.facetfaces > 0                     -- avoid division by zero
)
SELECT
    ma.equipment_type,
    SUM(CASE WHEN ma.is_high_fidelity THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS total_high_fidelity,
    COUNT(*)                                             AS total_meshes,
    ROUND(AVG(ma.mcr)::numeric,               2)         AS avg_mcr,
    ROUND(AVG(ma.facetresmm)::numeric,        2)         AS avg_mesh_resolution_mm,
    ROUND(AVG(ma.geomdeltamm)::numeric,       2)         AS avg_geometric_accuracy_mm
FROM mesh_analysis ma
GROUP BY ma.equipment_type
ORDER BY total_meshes DESC;

sol_sql:
/*
 * SQL Intent: Analyze environmental conditions at scanning sites to determine optimal scanning locations
 * Knowledge Used: #7 (Environmental Suitability Index - ESI), #15 (Optimal Scanning Conditions) #50
 * Advanced Functions: ABS(), POWER(), CASE expressions, Window functions (NTILE())
 */

-- Step 1: Calculate Environmental Suitability Index (ESI) for each environment record
WITH environment_analysis AS (
    SELECT 
        se.zoneref AS site_code,
        se.ambictemp,
        se.humepct,
        se.illumelux,
        -- Calculate ESI based on Knowledge #7
        100 - 2.5 * ABS(se.ambictemp - 20) - 
        POWER(ABS((se.humepct - 50) / 2), 1.5) - 
        600 / (se.illumelux + 100) AS esi
    FROM scanenvironment se
)

-- Step 2: Aggregate environmental metrics by site and classify scanning conditions
SELECT 
    s.zoneregistry AS site_code,
    s.zonelabel AS site_name,
    ROUND(AVG(ea.ambictemp)::numeric, 1) AS avg_temperature_c,
    ROUND(AVG(ea.humepct)::numeric, 1) AS avg_humidity_pct,
    ROUND(AVG(ea.illumelux)::numeric, 1) AS avg_illumination_lux,
    ROUND(AVG(ea.esi)::numeric, 1) AS avg_esi,
    -- Categorize sites by environmental suitability quartile
    NTILE(4) OVER (ORDER BY AVG(ea.esi)) AS esi_quartile,
    -- Classify scanning conditions based on Knowledge #15
    CASE 
        WHEN AVG(ea.esi) > 85 THEN 'Optimal Scanning Conditions'
        WHEN AVG(ea.esi) > 70 THEN 'Good Scanning Conditions'
        WHEN AVG(ea.esi) > 50 THEN 'Acceptable Scanning Conditions'
        ELSE 'Challenging Scanning Conditions'
    END AS scanning_condition_class,
FROM sites s
JOIN environment_analysis ea ON s.zoneregistry = ea.site_code
GROUP BY s.zoneregistry, s.zonelabel
ORDER BY avg_esi DESC;

follow_up.sol_sql:
/* ---------------------------------------------------------------
 * Purpose : From the sites that qualified as “Optimal Scanning Conditions”
 *           in the previous result, show how many distinct scans
 *           have been carried out and the time-span these scans cover.
 * Knowledge used : #7 (ESI), #15 (Optimal Scanning Conditions), #50 (ECCS)
 * Advanced SQL   : ABS(), POWER(), DATE functions, window agg., TO_CHAR
 * --------------------------------------------------------------- */
WITH environment_analysis AS (      -- 1. compute ESI for every record
    SELECT 
        se.zoneref                          AS site_code,
        se.ambictemp,
        se.humepct,
        se.illumelux,
        100
        - 2.5 * ABS(se.ambictemp - 20)
        - POWER(ABS((se.humepct - 50) / 2), 1.5)
        - 600 / (se.illumelux + 100)        AS esi          -- KB-7
    FROM scanenvironment  se
),
site_esi AS (                       -- 2. average ESI per site
    SELECT 
        s.zoneregistry               AS site_code,
        s.zonelabel                  AS site_name,
        AVG(ea.esi)                  AS avg_esi
    FROM sites               s
    JOIN environment_analysis ea
          ON ea.site_code = s.zoneregistry
    GROUP BY s.zoneregistry, s.zonelabel
),
optimal_sites AS (                  -- 3. keep only “Optimal” (>85, KB-15/50)
    SELECT *
    FROM site_esi
    WHERE avg_esi > 85
)
SELECT
    os.site_code,
    os.site_name,
    ROUND(os.avg_esi, 1)                       AS avg_esi,
    COUNT(DISTINCT sc.questregistry)           AS total_scans,
    TO_CHAR(MIN(sc.chronotag), 'YYYY-MM-DD')   AS first_scan_date,
    TO_CHAR(MAX(sc.chronotag), 'YYYY-MM-DD')   AS last_scan_date
FROM optimal_sites     os
LEFT JOIN scans        sc  ON sc.zoneref = os.site_code
GROUP BY os.site_code, os.site_name, os.avg_esi
ORDER BY total_scans DESC, os.avg_esi DESC;

sol_sql:
/*
 * SQL Intent: Analyze the efficiency of scan processing workflows to identify potential bottlenecks
 * Knowledge Used: #8 (Processing Efficiency Ratio - PER), #17 (Processing Bottleneck)
 * Advanced Functions: LOG(), GROUP BY, CASE expression
 */

-- Step 1: Calculate Processing Efficiency Ratio (PER) for each workflow based on Knowledge #8
SELECT 
    sp.flowsoft AS processing_software,
    sp.flowstage AS processing_stage,
    -- Calculate average processing metrics
    ROUND(AVG(sp.flowhrs)::numeric, 1) AS avg_processing_hours,
    ROUND(AVG(sp.proccpu)::numeric, 0) AS avg_cpu_usage_pct,
    ROUND(AVG(sp.procgpu)::numeric, 0) AS avg_gpu_usage_pct,
    ROUND(AVG(s.gbsize)::numeric, 1) AS avg_data_size_gb,
    -- Calculate PER based on Knowledge #8
    ROUND(
        AVG(
            s.gbsize * LOG(10, COALESCE(spc.totalpts, 1000000)) /
            (sp.flowhrs * (sp.proccpu + sp.procgpu) / 200)
        )::numeric,
        1
    ) AS avg_per,
    -- Identify bottlenecks based on Knowledge #17
    CASE 
        WHEN AVG(
            s.gbsize * LOG(10, COALESCE(spc.totalpts, 1000000)) /
            (sp.flowhrs * (sp.proccpu + sp.procgpu) / 200)
        ) < 0.5 THEN 'Bottleneck Detected'
        ELSE 'Efficient'
    END AS efficiency_status,
    -- Count workflows
    COUNT(*) AS workflow_count
FROM scanprocessing sp
JOIN scans s ON sp.zoneref = s.zoneref
LEFT JOIN scanpointcloud spc ON s.arcref = spc.arcref
WHERE sp.flowhrs > 0 AND (sp.proccpu + sp.procgpu) > 0
GROUP BY sp.flowsoft, sp.flowstage
ORDER BY 
    CASE 
        WHEN AVG(
            s.gbsize * LOG(10, COALESCE(spc.totalpts, 1000000)) /
            (sp.flowhrs * (sp.proccpu + sp.procgpu) / 200)
        ) < 0.5 THEN 1
        ELSE 2
    END,
    avg_per;

follow_up.sol_sql:
/*
 * SQL intent: extend the previous efficiency report by
 *   • adding memory- and storage-related metrics
 *   • calculating Processing Resource Utilization (PRU)           — KB #37
 *   • classifying PRU with Workflow Efficiency Classification     — KB #51
 *   • keeping the PER-based bottleneck flag from the first query  — KB #17
 */

WITH per_pru_base AS (
    SELECT
        sp.flowsoft,
        sp.flowstage,
        sp.flowhrs,
        sp.proccpu,
        sp.procgpu,
        sp.memusagegb,
        sp.remaingb,
        s.gbsize,
        COALESCE(spc.totalpts, 1e6)        AS totalpts,
        COALESCE(sm.facetverts, 1e6)       AS facetverts
    FROM scanprocessing           sp
    JOIN scans                    s   ON s.zoneref  = sp.zoneref
    LEFT JOIN scanpointcloud      spc ON spc.arcref = s.arcref
    LEFT JOIN scanmesh            sm  ON sm.zoneref = sp.zoneref
                                     AND sm.equipref = sp.equipref
    WHERE sp.flowhrs > 0
      AND (sp.proccpu + sp.procgpu) > 0
)
, metrics AS (
    SELECT
        flowsoft,
        flowstage,
        flowhrs,
        proccpu,
        procgpu,
        memusagegb,
        remaingb,
        gbsize,

        /*  PER — KB #8 */
        ( gbsize * LOG(10, totalpts)
          / ( flowhrs * (proccpu + procgpu) / 200.0 )
        )                                           AS per,

        /*  PRU — KB #37 */
        ( flowhrs * (proccpu + procgpu) / 2.0 )
        / ( gbsize * 10 * LOG(10, facetverts + 1e4) ) AS pru
    FROM per_pru_base
)
SELECT
    flowsoft  AS processing_software,
    flowstage AS processing_stage,

    ROUND(AVG(flowhrs)     ,1) AS avg_processing_hours,
    ROUND(AVG(proccpu)     ,0) AS avg_cpu_usage_pct,
    ROUND(AVG(procgpu)     ,0) AS avg_gpu_usage_pct,
    ROUND(AVG(gbsize)      ,1) AS avg_data_size_gb,

    ROUND(AVG(per) ,2) AS avg_per,
    /*  PER < 0.5 → Processing Bottleneck — KB #17  */
    CASE
        WHEN AVG(per) < 0.5 THEN 'Bottleneck Detected'
        ELSE                     'Efficient'
    END AS efficiency_status,
    COUNT(*) AS workflow_count,

    ROUND(AVG(memusagegb)  ,1) AS avg_memory_usage_gb,
    ROUND(AVG(remaingb)    ,1) AS avg_remaining_storage_gb,
    ROUND(AVG(pru) ,2) AS avg_pru,
    /*  Workflow Efficiency Classification — KB #51 */
    CASE
        WHEN AVG(pru) <  5.0 THEN 'Optimized'
        WHEN AVG(pru) <= 10.0 THEN 'Acceptable'
        ELSE                     'Needs Optimization'
    END AS workflow_efficiency

FROM metrics
GROUP BY flowsoft, flowstage
ORDER BY
    CASE WHEN AVG(per) < 0.5 THEN 1 ELSE 2 END;   -- bottlenecks first

sol_sql:
/*
 * SQL Intent: Identify sites with high potential for feature extraction based on point cloud characteristics.
 * Knowledge Used: #2 (Point Cloud Density Ratio - PCDR), #32 (Feature Extraction Efficiency - FEE)
 * Advanced Functions: POWER(), NULLIF(), ROUND()
 * Difficulty: Normal
 */
WITH feature_potential AS (
    -- Step 1: Calculate Point Cloud Density Ratio (PCDR) for each scan, using TotalPts, CloudDense, and AreaM2 from relevant tables.
    SELECT
        s.zoneref AS site_code,
        spc.totalpts,
        spc.clouddense,
        ss.aream2,
        CASE
            WHEN spc.clouddense > 0 AND ss.aream2 > 0 THEN spc.totalpts / (spc.clouddense * ss.aream2)
            ELSE 0  -- Handle cases where division by zero might occur
        END AS pcdr
    FROM sites si
    LEFT JOIN scans s ON si.zoneregistry = s.zoneref
    LEFT JOIN scanpointcloud spc ON s.arcref = spc.arcref
    LEFT JOIN scanspatial ss ON s.arcref = ss.arcref
),
extraction_metrics AS (
    -- Step 2: Calculate Feature Extraction Efficiency (FEE) using PCDR and feature counts from scanfeatures.
    SELECT
        fp.site_code,
        fp.pcdr,
        sf.traitcount,
        sf.articount,
        CASE
            WHEN fp.pcdr > 0 THEN (sf.traitcount + sf.articount) / (fp.pcdr * SQRT(fp.clouddense)) * 1000
            ELSE 0  -- Handle cases where division by zero might occur
        END AS fee
    FROM feature_potential fp
    JOIN scanfeatures sf ON fp.site_code = sf.zoneref
)
-- Step 3: Aggregate and present the results, calculating average PCDR and FEE for each site.
SELECT
    site_code,
    ROUND(AVG(pcdr)::numeric, 2) AS avg_pcdr,
    ROUND(AVG(fee)::numeric, 2) AS avg_fee
FROM extraction_metrics
GROUP BY site_code
ORDER BY avg_fee DESC;

follow_up.sol_sql:
/*
 * Adds Environmental Suitability Index (ESI) and ECCS tier to the
 * per-site metrics computed in the first round.
 * New knowledge used:
 *   #7  – Environmental Suitability Index (ESI)
 *   #50 – Environmental Condition Classification System (ECCS)
 */

WITH feature_potential AS (              -- identical to 1st-round CTE
    SELECT
        s.zoneref                    AS site_code,
        spc.totalpts,
        spc.clouddense,
        ss.aream2,
        CASE
            WHEN spc.clouddense > 0
              AND ss.aream2     > 0  THEN spc.totalpts / (spc.clouddense * ss.aream2)
            ELSE 0
        END                          AS pcdr
    FROM   sites si
    LEFT   JOIN scans           s  ON si.zoneregistry = s.zoneref
    LEFT   JOIN scanpointcloud  spc ON s.arcref = spc.arcref
    LEFT   JOIN scanspatial     ss  ON s.arcref = ss.arcref
),
extraction_metrics AS (                  -- identical to 1st-round CTE
    SELECT
        fp.site_code,
        fp.pcdr,
        fp.clouddense,
        sf.traitcount,
        sf.articount,
        CASE
            WHEN fp.pcdr > 0 THEN
                (sf.traitcount + sf.articount) /
                (fp.pcdr * SQRT(fp.clouddense)) * 1000
            ELSE 0
        END                            AS fee
    FROM   feature_potential fp
    JOIN   scanfeatures      sf ON fp.site_code = sf.zoneref
),
env_readings AS (                        -- compute ESI for every environment record
    SELECT
        se.zoneref              AS site_code,
        100
          - 2.5 * ABS(se.ambictemp - 20)
          - POWER(ABS((se.humepct - 50) / 2.0), 1.5)
          - 600.0 / (se.illumelux + 100)        AS esi
    FROM   scanenvironment se
),
env_site_avg AS (                        -- average ESI per site
    SELECT site_code,
           AVG(esi) AS avg_esi
    FROM   env_readings
    GROUP  BY site_code
),
site_metrics AS (                        -- pull everything together
    SELECT
        em.site_code,
        ROUND(AVG(em.pcdr)::numeric, 2)      AS avg_pcdr,
        ROUND(AVG(em.fee )::numeric, 2)      AS avg_fee,
        ROUND(COALESCE(es.avg_esi, 0)::numeric, 2) AS avg_esi
    FROM   extraction_metrics em
    LEFT   JOIN env_site_avg es ON em.site_code = es.site_code
    GROUP  BY em.site_code, es.avg_esi
)
SELECT
    site_code,
    avg_pcdr,
    avg_fee,
    avg_esi,
    CASE                                            -- ECCS tier (#50)
        WHEN avg_esi > 85 THEN 'Optimal Scanning Conditions'
        WHEN avg_esi > 70 THEN 'Good Scanning Conditions'
        WHEN avg_esi > 50 THEN 'Acceptable Scanning Conditions'
        ELSE                        'Challenging Scanning Conditions'
    END AS scanning_condition_tier
FROM   site_metrics
ORDER  BY avg_fee DESC;

sol_sql:
/* Intent: Analyze processing efficiency and resource utilization
   Knowledge Used: #37 (Processing Resource Utilization), #51
   Advanced Features: Advanced aggregation, JSON functions, Window functions
*/

WITH resource_metrics AS (
    -- Step 1: Calculate resource utilization metrics
    SELECT 
        sp.flowregistry,
        sp.equipref,
        sp.flowhrs,
        sp.proccpu,
        sp.procgpu,
        sp.remaingb,
        sm.facetfaces,
        sm.facetverts,
        -- Calculate PRU
        sp.flowhrs * (sp.proccpu + sp.procgpu) / 2.0 / 
        (sp.remaingb * 10 * LOG(sm.facetverts + 10000)) as pru
    FROM scanprocessing sp
    LEFT JOIN scanmesh sm ON sp.zoneref = sm.zoneref
),
workflow_analysis AS (
    -- Step 2: Analyze workflow efficiency
    SELECT 
        rm.equipref,
        rm.pru,
        ROUND(AVG(rm.flowhrs) OVER (PARTITION BY rm.equipref), 2) as avg_processing_hours,
        DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY rm.pru) as efficiency_rank,
        JSON_BUILD_OBJECT(
            'cpu_usage', rm.proccpu,
            'gpu_usage', rm.procgpu,
            'processing_hours', rm.flowhrs
        ) as resource_details
    FROM resource_metrics rm
)
SELECT 
    equipref,
    ROUND(pru::numeric, 2) as processing_resource_utilization,
    avg_processing_hours,
    efficiency_rank,
    CASE 
        WHEN pru < 5.0 THEN 'Optimized'
        WHEN pru < 10.0 THEN 'Acceptable'
        ELSE 'Needs Optimization'
    END as workflow_status,
    resource_details
FROM workflow_analysis
ORDER BY pru ASC;

/* 
 * SQL Intent: Create materialized view for high fidelity meshes
 * Knowledge: #4 (Mesh Complexity Ratio), #13 (High Fidelity Mesh)
 * Advanced Features: Materialized view, Function index
 */

CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW high_fidelity_meshes AS
SELECT
    sm.facetregistry,
    sm.zoneref,
    sm.equipref,
    sm.facetverts,
    sm.facetfaces,
    sm.facetresmm,
    sm.geomdeltamm,
    -- Calculate MCR per knowledge #4
    (sm.facetfaces::float / NULLIF(sm.facetverts, 0) / POWER(sm.facetresmm, 2)) * 1000 AS mcr
FROM scanmesh sm
WHERE 
    -- Apply High Fidelity Mesh criteria from knowledge #13
    (sm.facetfaces::float / NULLIF(sm.facetverts, 0) / POWER(sm.facetresmm, 2)) * 1000 > 5.0 AND
    sm.facetresmm < 1.0 AND
    sm.geomdeltamm < 0.5;

sol_sql:
-- Intent: Define a reusable function to calculate the SRI metric for scan quality assessment.
-- KB Used: 0 (Scan Resolution Index - SRI)
-- Advanced Features: CREATE FUNCTION, Mathematical functions (LOG), Parameter handling

-- Step 1: Define the function signature, specifying input parameters (resolution, density) and return type.
-- Step 2: Implement the SRI formula from KB 0 using PostgreSQL's mathematical functions. Handle potential division by zero or invalid log input.
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION calculate_sri(scan_resolution_mm NUMERIC, point_density INTEGER)
RETURNS NUMERIC AS $$
BEGIN
    -- Step 2a: Check for invalid inputs to avoid errors (e.g., non-positive values for log).
    IF scan_resolution_mm <= 0 OR point_density <= 0 THEN
        RETURN NULL; -- Or raise an error, or return a specific value indicating invalid input
    END IF;
    -- Step 2b: Calculate SRI using the formula. Use NULLIF to prevent division by zero if log(point_density) is zero.
    RETURN (LOG(10, scan_resolution_mm * 1000) / NULLIF(LOG(10, point_density), 0)) * 5;
EXCEPTION
    -- Step 2c: Handle potential math errors (e.g., log of non-positive number if initial check missed something).
    WHEN numeric_value_out_of_range OR division_by_zero THEN
        RETURN NULL; -- Return NULL if calculation fails
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql IMMUTABLE; -- Mark as IMMUTABLE if output depends only on inputs

-- Intent: Provide an easy way to query scans meeting the criteria for 'Premium Quality'.
-- KB Used: KB 10 (High Resolution), KB 11 (Comprehensive Coverage)
-- Advanced Features: CREATE VIEW, JOIN (LEFT JOIN), Multiple conditions

-- Step 1: Define the view structure.
-- Step 2: Join scans and scanpointcloud tables to access necessary metrics.
-- Step 3: Apply the criteria from KB 10 (Resolution, Density) and KB 11 (Coverage, Overlap).
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW view_premium_quality_scans AS
SELECT
    s.QuestRegistry,
    s.ArcRef,
    s.ZoneRef,
    s.ChronoTag,
    spc.ScanResolMm,
    spc.PointDense,
    spc.CoverPct,
    spc.LapPct,
    spc.NoiseDb
FROM
    public.scans s
LEFT JOIN -- Use LEFT JOIN in case some scans don't have point cloud data yet
    public.scanpointcloud spc ON s.ArcRef = spc.ArcRef AND s.CrewRef = spc.CrewRef -- Adjust join keys as needed
WHERE
    -- Step 3a: Apply High Resolution criteria (KB 10)
    spc.ScanResolMm <= 1.0 AND spc.PointDense >= 1000
    -- Step 3b: Apply Comprehensive Coverage criteria (KB 11)
    AND spc.CoverPct >= 95.0 AND spc.LapPct >= 30.0
    -- Step 3c: Add a basic noise check
    AND spc.NoiseDb < 1.5; -- Example threshold for low noise

sol_sql:
/* Intent: Delete redundant scans while keeping those with best quality (SQS) and ensuring comprehensive coverage
Knowledge used: 
- #3 (Scan Quality Score): SQS = (10/SRI)^1.5 × (SCE/100) × (1 - NoiseDb/30)^2
- #11 (Comprehensive Coverage): CoverPct ≥ 95 AND LapPct ≥ 30
Advanced features: DELETE with CTEs, Window functions, Multiple-step calculations */

DELETE FROM scans
USING (
    WITH scan_metrics AS (
        -- Step 1: Calculate SRI (Scan Resolution Index) 
        SELECT s.questregistry,
               s.arcref,
               sp.scanresolmm,
               sp.pointdense,
               sp.coverpct,
               sp.lappct,
               sp.noisedb,
               (LOG(10, sp.scanresolmm * 1000) / LOG(10, sp.pointdense) * 5) AS sri
        FROM scans s
        JOIN scanpointcloud sp USING (arcref)
        -- Filter 1: Only consider scans that don't meet comprehensive coverage standard
        WHERE sp.coverpct < 95 OR sp.lappct < 30
    ),
    quality_scores AS (
        -- Step 2: Calculate full SQS incorporating both SCE and noise factors
        SELECT questregistry,
               arcref,
               coverpct,
               lappct,
               -- Complete SQS calculation per Knowledge #3
               POWER(10/sri, 1.5) * 
               ((coverpct * (1 + lappct/100 * (1 - coverpct/100)))/100) *
               POWER(1 - noisedb/30, 2) AS sqs,
               -- Rank scans within each project based on quality
               ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
                   PARTITION BY arcref 
                   ORDER BY 
                       -- First prioritize scans meeting coverage standards
                       CASE WHEN coverpct >= 95 AND lappct >= 30 THEN 1 
                            ELSE 2 END,
                       -- Then order by SQS for quality
                       POWER(10/sri, 1.5) * 
                       ((coverpct * (1 + lappct/100 * (1 - coverpct/100)))/100) *
                       POWER(1 - noisedb/30, 2) DESC
               ) AS quality_rank
        FROM scan_metrics
    )
    -- Step 3: Select redundant scans for deletion
    SELECT questregistry
    FROM quality_scores
    WHERE quality_rank > 1  -- Keep only the best scan per project
) redundant
WHERE scans.questregistry = redundant.questregistry
RETURNING scans.questregistry, scans.arcref;

                   -- Then order by SQS for quality
                   POWER(10/sri, 1.5) * 
                   ((coverpct * (1 + lappct/100 * (1 - coverpct/100)))/100) * 
                   POWER(1 - noisedb/30, 2) DESC
           ) AS quality_rank
    FROM scan_metrics
)
-- Step 3: Select the details of the kept scan
SELECT qs.questregistry,
       qs.arcref,
       qs.sqs,
       qs.coverpct,
       qs.lappct
FROM quality_scores qs
WHERE qs.quality_rank = 1; -- Select the best scan (rank 1)

WITH HighRiskFlows AS (
    SELECT 
        df.RecordRegistry, 
        COALESCE(rm.RiskAssess / NULLIF(rm.CtrlEff, 0), 0) AS res, 
        COALESCE(dp.VolGB, 0) * 
            CASE 
                WHEN dp.DataSense = 'High' THEN 3 
                WHEN dp.DataSense = 'Medium' THEN 2 
                ELSE 1 
            END AS dsi
    FROM DataFlow df
    LEFT JOIN RiskManagement rm 
        ON df.RecordRegistry = rm.FlowLink
    LEFT JOIN DataProfile dp 
        ON df.RecordRegistry = dp.FlowSign
    WHERE COALESCE(rm.RiskAssess / NULLIF(rm.CtrlEff, 0), 0) > 0.7 
        AND COALESCE(dp.VolGB, 0) * 
            CASE 
                WHEN dp.DataSense = 'High' THEN 3 
                WHEN dp.DataSense = 'Medium' THEN 2 
                ELSE 1 
            END > 100
),
RankedFlows AS (
    SELECT 
        RecordRegistry, 
        res, 
        dsi, 
        RANK() OVER (ORDER BY dsi DESC) AS volume_rank
    FROM HighRiskFlows
)
SELECT 
    RecordRegistry, 
    ROUND(res::numeric, 2) AS risk_exposure_score, 
    ROUND(dsi::numeric, 2) AS data_sensitivity_index
FROM RankedFlows
WHERE volume_rank <= 5
ORDER BY volume_rank
LIMIT 5;

WITH VendorRisk AS (
    SELECT 
        vm.VendorTrace, 
        CASE 
            WHEN vm.VendSecRate = 'A' THEN 4 
            WHEN vm.VendSecRate = 'B' THEN 3 
            WHEN vm.VendSecRate = 'C' THEN 2 
            ELSE 1 
        END * 
        CASE 
            WHEN vm.ContrState = 'Active' THEN 1 
            ELSE 0.5 
        END AS vri
    FROM VendorManagement vm
),
TieredVendors AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN vri < 2 THEN 'High Risk' 
            WHEN vri < 3 THEN 'Medium Risk' 
            ELSE 'Low Risk' 
        END AS risk_tier
    FROM VendorRisk
)
SELECT 
    risk_tier, 
    COUNT(*) AS vendor_count, 
    ROUND(COUNT(*) * 100.0 / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER (), 2) AS percentage
FROM TieredVendors
GROUP BY risk_tier
ORDER BY vendor_count DESC LIMIT 100

WITH FlowMetrics AS (
    SELECT 
        df.RecordRegistry, 
        df.BwidthPct * df.DataSizeMB / df.DurMin AS bsi, 
        COALESCE(df.SuccessPct / NULLIF(df.ErrTally + 1, 0), 0) AS dte
    FROM DataFlow df
    WHERE df.BwidthPct * df.DataSizeMB / df.DurMin > 50 
        AND COALESCE(df.SuccessPct / NULLIF(df.ErrTally + 1, 0), 0) < 1.0
)
SELECT 
    RecordRegistry, 
    ROUND(bsi::numeric, 2) AS bandwidth_saturation_index, 
    ROUND(dte::numeric, 2) AS data_transfer_efficiency
FROM FlowMetrics
ORDER BY bsi DESC
LIMIT 10

WITH FlowMetrics AS (
    SELECT 
        df.RecordRegistry, 
        df.OrigNation,
        df.DestNation,
        df.BwidthPct * df.DataSizeMB / df.DurMin AS bsi, 
        COALESCE(df.SuccessPct / NULLIF(df.ErrTally + 1, 0), 0) AS dte,
        COALESCE(df.SuccessPct / NULLIF(df.ErrTally + 1, 0), 0) * (1 - df.RtryTally::numeric / NULLIF(df.ErrTally + 1, 0)) * (df.SuccessPct / NULLIF(df.ErrTally + 1, 0)) AS dfsi
    FROM DataFlow df
    WHERE df.BwidthPct * df.DataSizeMB / df.DurMin > 50 
        AND COALESCE(df.SuccessPct / NULLIF(df.ErrTally + 1, 0), 0) < 1.0
)
SELECT 
    RecordRegistry, 
    CONCAT(OrigNation, ' -> ', DestNation) AS transfer_path,
    ROUND(dfsi::numeric, 2) AS data_flow_stability_index
FROM FlowMetrics
ORDER BY bsi DESC
LIMIT 10;

WITH NonCompliantFlows AS (
    SELECT 
        com.ComplianceTrace,
        com.GdprComp,
        com.LocLawComp,
        df.OrigNation,
        df.DestNation
    FROM Compliance com
    RIGHT JOIN DataFlow df 
        ON com.RecordRegistry = df.RecordRegistry
    WHERE EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 
        FROM DataFlow df2 
        WHERE df2.RecordRegistry = df.RecordRegistry 
        AND df2.OrigNation != df2.DestNation
    )
    AND (
        com.GdprComp = 'Non-compliant' 
        OR com.LocLawComp = 'Non-compliant'
    )
)
SELECT 
    ComplianceTrace,
    GdprComp,
    LocLawComp,
    OrigNation || ' -> ' || DestNation AS transfer_path
FROM NonCompliantFlows
ORDER BY ComplianceTrace
LIMIT 10

        dp.VolGB,
        rm.RiskAssess,
        rm.CtrlEff
    FROM Compliance com
    RIGHT JOIN DataFlow df 
        ON com.RecordRegistry = df.RecordRegistry
    LEFT JOIN DataProfile dp 
        ON df.RecordRegistry = dp.RecordRegistry
    LEFT JOIN RiskManagement rm 
        ON df.RecordRegistry = rm.RecordRegistry
    WHERE EXISTS (

        FROM DataFlow df2 
        WHERE df2.RecordRegistry = df.RecordRegistry 
        AND df2.OrigNation != df2.DestNation

    )

    )::numeric, 2) AS cross_border_compliance_exposure
FROM NonCompliantFlows
ORDER BY ComplianceTrace
LIMIT 10;

WITH AuditSeverity AS (
    SELECT 
        ac.AuditTrace,
        COALESCE(ac.CritFindNum::float / NULLIF(ac.FindTally + 1, 0), 0) AS afs,
        ac.RemedDue
    FROM AuditAndCompliance ac
    WHERE ac.RemedDue IS NOT NULL
),
MonthlyTrends AS (
    SELECT 
        TO_CHAR(RemedDue, 'YYYY-MM') AS audit_month,
        AVG(afs) AS avg_severity,
        DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(afs) DESC) AS severity_rank
    FROM AuditSeverity
    WHERE afs > 0.5
    GROUP BY TO_CHAR(RemedDue, 'YYYY-MM')
)
SELECT 
    audit_month,
    ROUND(avg_severity::numeric, 2) AS average_afs,
    severity_rank
FROM MonthlyTrends
WHERE severity_rank <= 3
ORDER BY audit_month
